name: request-refactor-plan
tagline: Interviews the user to scope a refactor, then files the resulting plan as a GitHub issue using a fixed template.
verdict: No security concerns. But it declares no out-of-scope cases, lets the agent skip steps with no criteria (non-deterministic), has no failure path for GitHub issue creation, can duplicate issues on rerun, and lacks tool declaration or versioning.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for' or out-of-scope guidance anywhere in description or body.
Step 8 creates a GitHub issue with no stated fallback if gh/GitHub is unavailable or unauthenticated.
Line 6: 'You may skip steps if you don't consider them necessary' with no criteria — agents will diverge.
